candidate_problem_record.subject: 物理
candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: Four identical coherent monochromatic wave sources A, B, C, D, as shown in Fig. 4.2 produce waves of the same wavelength $\lambda$. Two receivers $R_{1}$ and $R_{2}$ are at great (but equal) distances from $B$. Which receiver picks up the greater signal?
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image object representation rather than a file path or URL
metadata.reasoning_chain: Let $r$ be the distance of $R_{1}$ and $R_{2}$ from $B$. We are given $r \gg \lambda$. Suppose the amplitude of the electric vector of the electromagnetic waves emitted by each source is $E_{0}$. The total amplitudes of the electric field at the receivers are $R_{1}: \; E_{10}= E_{0} \exp\left[i K\left(r-\frac{\lambda}{2}\right)\right] + E_{0} e^{i K r} + E_{0} \exp\left[i K\left(r+\frac{\lambda}{2}\right)\right] + E_{0} \exp\left[i K \sqrt{r^{2}+\frac{\lambda^{2}}{4}}\right]$, $R_{2}: \; E_{20}= E_{0} e^{i K r} + E_{0} \exp\left[i K\left(r+\frac{\lambda}{2}\right)\right] + 2 E_{0} \exp\left[i K \sqrt{r^{2}+\frac{\lambda^{2}}{4}}\right]$. As $K\lambda=2\pi$, $\exp\left[\pm i \frac{K \lambda}{2}\right]=e^{\pm i\pi}=-1$. With $r \gg \lambda$, $\sqrt{r^{2}+\frac{\lambda^{2}}{4}} \approx r$. Thus $E_{0} \exp\left[i K \sqrt{r^{2}+\frac{\lambda^{2}}{4}\right] \approx E_{0} e^{i K r}$ and we have $E_{10} \approx 0$, $E_{20} \approx 2 E_{0} e^{i K r}$. The intensity of a signal $I \propto |E|^{2}$, so the intensities received by $R_{1}$ and $R_{2}$ are respectively $I_{0}=0$, $I_{2} \sim 4 E_{0}^{2}$. Hence $R_{2}$ picks up greater signal.
